title: Complex Noun Phrases
explain:
Complex noun phrases can include pre-modifiers (adjectives, nouns, participles before the noun) and post-modifiers (prepositional phrases, relative clauses, infinitives, participles after the noun). Order of adjectives: opinion→size→age→shape→colour→origin→material→purpose + NOUN. In academic writing, noun phrases often carry most of the information: 'the rapid economic development of East Asia in the 1980s'.

Key rules:
- Adjective order: opinion, size, age, shape, colour, origin, material
- Pre-modifiers: participle phrases before noun: 'the recently published study'
- Post-modifiers: PP or relative clause after noun: 'the study published last year'
- Nominalised heads: 'an increase in...', 'a decline in...'

questions.q: She bought a ___ vase at the auction.
The ___ results were published in a major journal.
He is a ___ student who always gets top marks.
The house ___ the corner is the oldest in the city.
We need a ___ solution to this problem.
She is the only person ___ knows the truth.
We were impressed by the ___ of the presentation.
It was a ___ decision that affected everyone.
The ___ report was 200 pages long.
They found several ___ artifacts during the dig.
She is the only person ___ knows the truth. (V2)
The ___ report was 200 pages long. (V2)
The house ___ the corner is the oldest in the city. (V2)
She is the only person ___ knows the truth. (V2)
The ___ results were published in a major journal. (V2)
He is a ___ student who always gets top marks. (V2)
They found several ___ artifacts during the dig. (V2)
It was a ___ decision that affected everyone. (V2)
She is the only person ___ knows the truth. (V2)
The ___ report was 200 pages long. (V2)

examples.wrong: The published recently report attracted attention.
examples.correct: The recently published report attracted attention.

examples.wrong: The results of the experiment which we conducted it last year
examples.correct: The results of the experiment conducted last year
examples.note: Reduced relative: omit 'which we conducted it'
